data.title: Why Is Powell Max Stock (PMAX) Down Today?
data.description:
Powell Max (PMAX) stock plummeted on Friday after the financial communications and financial printing services company effected a secondary offering of its shares. This included 74,809,231 Class A ordinary shares held by selling shareholders. Powell Max has agreed to sell these shares on behalf of the selling shareholders.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Powell Max noted that it won't see any proceeds from the sale of these shares. The company also warned that the secondary offering could have a significant negative impact on PMAX stock, which explains why the shares have fallen today.

Powell Max noted that the shares included in this secondary offering come from the company's private placement in January 2025. Selling shareholders have the right to sell all or some of their shares and can do so across public or private transactions at prevailing market prices, at prices related to prevailing market prices, or at privately negotiated prices.

Powell Max Stock Sinks Today

Powell Max stock was down 31.18% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 3.63% fall yesterday. The shares have rallied 2.76% year-to-date but were down 39.14% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of PMAX stock, as some 106,000 shares changed hands. That's well above the company's three-month average daily trading volume of about 38,980 shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: Ahead of Q1 Earnings, A Look at Who Owns Adobe Stock (ADBE)
data.description:
Adobe (ADBE) is set to release its first-quarter results for FY26 on March 12. So far this year, ADBE stock has dropped roughly 20%. In this article, we break down who owns ADBE and what it could mean for investors. The company's ownership structure provides valuable insight into both confidence and caution surrounding this software stock ahead of earnings.

data.title: Stock Futures Plunge as Oil Prices Spike amid U.S.-Iran Conflict
data.description:
Stock futures fell on Friday morning as oil prices surged amid escalating U.S.-Iran tensions. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.76%, 0.56%, and 0.54%, respectively, at 8:36 a.m. EST on March 6.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the time of writing, benchmark Brent crude futures rose 3.6% to trade above $88 per barrel. Meanwhile, West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude futures soared 6%, trading at $85.81 per barrel. The U.S.-Iran war has entered its seventh day and continues to disrupt global fuel supplies. 

Qatar's energy minister told The Financial Times that Gulf energy producers may have to shut down production in a move that could push oil to $150 a barrel. He cautioned that the U.S.-Iran conflict could "bring down the economies of the world."

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major averages ended in the red due to geopolitical concerns. The S&P 500, the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite, and the Dow Jones fell 0.56%, 1.61%, and 0.26%, respectively. Overall, the Dow Jones is heading towards its second consecutive week of losses.

Turning to trending stocks, Marvell Technology (MRVL) shares rallied 11% in pre-market trading after the semiconductor company ai-demand">reported solid results and outlook, driven by AI-led demand. In contrast, Gap (GAP) stock was down more 9% after the apparel company missed Q4 earnings expectations.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: U.S. Airlines Could be Forced to Hike Prices after Taking $5.8B Fuel Bill Hit From Iran War
data.description:
U.S. airlines and their passengers could be set for a nasty financial shock from the hike in oil prices as a result of the Iran war.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Hedging Your Bets

The price of Brent Crude has rocketed 23% in the past month, driven most recently by Iran pounding the Gulf states in retaliation for the U.S. and Israeli attack last weekend.

Airlines in the States are particularly vulnerable to an increase in jet fuel prices because they, according to a Reuters article, ceased hedging them over the past two decades. That's huge given that fuel is the second-largest expense for air carriers after labor.

Hedging can protect airlines from spikes in fuel costs through the ⁠use of derivative contracts. But it can also backfire when prices fall, exposing carriers to above-market rates.

Reuters reported that without hedging, airlines are exposed to a prolonged bump in jet fuel prices, now at $2.83 per gallon on average, according to the Oil Price Information Service. Spot fuel ​traded at the U.S. Gulf Coast surged to $4.12 a gallon yesterday, the highest since June 2022, according to Platts.

Higher Ticket Prices

Delta Air Lines (DAL) said in its annual filing that a one-cent increase in the cost of jet fuel per gallon would increase fuel expenses annually by about $40 million. For American Airlines (AAL) the increase would be about $50 million, and for Southwest (LUV) $22 million.

All told, those three U.S. carriers and United Airlines (UAL) are looking at a combined $5.8 billion in additional fuel costs if jet fuel prices remain at these elevated levels all year, according to Reuters calculations.

Airlines are also being impacted by the closure of airspace over the Gulf and what the war will do to already fragile consumer confidence. It is unlikely that travelers will be planning any trips to Dubai or Cyprus anytime soon.

If the conflict continues for weeks or months, then airlines are likely to take a serious hit to their bottom lines. That could also impact travelers with a hike in prices.

"I'm pretty convinced the airlines are going to remain unhedged in the U.S. and look ​to pass through the costs to end ​consumers (only if needed in the event of ⁠sustained fuel inflation) instead," Morgan Stanley analyst Ravi Shanker said.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Why Netflix Rejecting Warner Bros Discovery May Benefit Shareholders
data.description:
Sometimes the best acquisition strategy is knowing when to walk away. That appears to be exactly what Netflix (NFLX) has done. After months of speculation and bidding tension surrounding Warner Bros. Discovery's (WBD) streaming and studio assets, Netflix ultimately declined to raise its offer and stepped aside as Paramount Skydance (PSKY) moves forward with a higher proposal.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At first glance, that decision might look like a missed opportunity. Warner's iconic content library and film studio would have been a powerful addition to Netflix's ecosystem. But for shareholders, the refusal to chase the deal could ultimately prove far more valuable than winning it.

In fact, Netflix's decision highlights something investors often overlook during headline-grabbing acquisition battles: disciplined capital allocation can create more value than scale. In light of this discipline and the company's refusal to overpay, I remain bullish on NFLX stock.
data.author: Davit Kirakosyan

data.title: Rezolve AI Stock (RZLV) Could Soar 300+%: Here's Why Wall Street Is Excited
data.description:
Shares in Rezolve AI (RZLV), the London-based provider of AI solutions for commerce, have only gained about 6% since the start of the year. However, analysts on Wall Street see the stock jumping about 314% over the next 12 months.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

What Does Rezolve AI Do?

Rezolve AI operates as an AI-first service desk, providing retailers, brands, and manufacturers with artificial intelligence-powered employee support as well as conversational and visual commerce capabilities.

The tool operates within productivity applications such as Microsoft's (MSFT) and Salesforce's (CRM) Slack to handle automated repetitive tasks such as resetting passwords, troubleshooting virtual private networks during downtimes, and fielding queries on HR policy. It can also connect to content systems such as SharePoint and Atlassian's (TEAM) Confluence to answer employee questions.

Furthermore, Rezolve AI's Brain Suite enterprise AI platform offers conversation-based or visual search shopping features to e-commerce platforms. The latter describes activities such as pointing a smartphone at a physical object to check for availability within a retailer's online catalog.

Analysts Cheer Rezolve AI's 67% Revenue Growth Forecast

In February, Rezolve AI agreed to buy out Reward Loyalty UK for $230 million, a move CEO Daniel Wagner believes "materially advances [the company's] core AI commerce strategy by embedding Rezolve AI deeper into everyday consumer spend across banks, retailers, and payment networks."

In recently reaffirming its Buy rating on Rezolve AI, investment firm Alliance Global pointed to the company's $209 million revenue in 2025 and plans to scale that by 67% to $350 million by the end of fiscal 2026. Alliance Global also raised its RZLV price target to $14 — which implied over 300% upside — noting that it sees continued adoption of the company's products.

Analyst Flags Acquisition Impact on Cash Balance

In another update, however, Alliance Global analyst Brian Kinstlinger — while still calling RZLV a Buy — trimmed his price target to $13, which implies about 378% upside. Kinstlinger pointed to the acquisition of Reward Loyalty UK, noting the impact on the company's cash balance.

Nonetheless, the five-star analyst remains bullish on Rezolve AI's projected 67% revenue jump.

Meanwhile, H.C. Wainwright also recently maintained its Buy rating on Rezolve AI and raised its price target to $12, implying 341.18% upside. The financial services firm noted that Rezolve AI's revenue forecast is almost double Wall Street's estimate.

H.C. Wainwright also believes that Rezolve AI's December profits marked a turning point for the business.

Is Rezolve AI a Good Stock to Buy?

Across Wall Street, analysts remain bullish on Rezolve AI's shares and have a Strong Buy rating on the stock. This is based on six Buys issued over the past three months.

In addition, the average RZLV price target of $11.25 implies 313.60% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Why Is Cre8 Enterprise Stock (CRE) Up Today?
data.description:
Cre8 Enterprise (CRE) stock surged on Friday after the Hong Kong-based financial printing services company announced it has avoided being delisted from the Nasdaq exchange. The company is now compliant with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5550(a)(2), which requires shares to trade above the $1 minimum bid price to be listed on the exchange.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Cre8 Enterprise was able to boost its share price above the $1 minimum via a reverse stock split. The company did so on a one-for-12 basis, which saw it consolidate 12 shares into a single share. This resulted in the company's shares trading above the $1 minimum for the required 10-day period, eliminating the Nasdaq complaint.

Cre8 Enterprise was first notified of its listing price issue in October 2025. The company was given until April 2026 to sort out the problem. Cre8 Enterprise's decision to enact a reverse stock split to boost its share price is one that many companies take when in similar situations. It's a quick fix, but one that can still leave the company's shares in danger if they continue to slide back toward the $1 minimum.

Cre8 Enterprise Stock Gains Today

Cre8 Enterprise stock was up 74.31% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 4.03% fall yesterday. The shares have decreased 5.59% year-to-date and 95.04% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of CRE stock, as more than 9.7 million shares changed hands. For perspective, the company's three-month daily average trading volume is about 3.05 million shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/6/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Fall on Escalating Iran Conflict, Surging Oil Prices
data.description:
Stock futures traded lower early Friday amid U.S.-Iran tensions and rising oil prices after an earlier drop that boosted sentiment in international stock markets. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.39%, 0.29%, and 0.21%, respectively, at 5:35 a.m. EST on March 6.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the time of writing, benchmark Brent crude futures were up 2.27% to $87.35 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate crude futures were 4% higher at $84.26 a barrel. The U.S.-Iran war has entered its seventh day and continues to disrupt global fuel supplies. 

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major averages ended in the red due to geopolitical concerns. The S&P 500, the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite, and the Dow Jones fell 0.56%, 1.61%, and 0.26%, respectively. Overall, the Dow Jones is heading towards its second consecutive week of losses.

Coming to economic releases, investors await the release of February's nonfarm payrolls report today. The market consensus indicates a notable slowdown to about 50,000 job additions, compared with 130,000 in January 2026.

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.167%. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price traded around $5,087 per ounce on Thursday.

Turning to trending stocks, Marvell Technology (MRVL) shares rallied about 12% in pre-market trading after the semiconductor company ai-demand">reported solid results and outlook, driven by AI-led demand. In contrast, Gap (GPS) stock was down more than 7% after the apparel company missed Q4 earnings expectations.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today but fell later as oil prices resumed their rally.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets rose on March 6, despite ongoing tensions in the Middle East. Investors continue to assess the impact of the U.S.-Iran conflict on energy prices.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rose 1.72%. In China, the Shanghai Composite gained 0.38%, while the Shenzhen Component increased 0.59%. 

Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei gained 0.62%, with the Topix up 0.39%.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju
